title: Wisdom and warning for the young
summary_text: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel. The chapter sets Solomon's purpose before the reader and warns the young not to join violent sinners. Wisdom is presented as a public call that must be heard before ruin comes. But whoso hearkeneth unto me shall dwell securely, And shall be quiet without fear of evil.
key_points: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel.
To know wisdom and instruction.
To discern the words of understanding.
To receive instruction in wise dealing, In righteousness and justice and equity.
But whoso hearkeneth unto me shall dwell securely, And shall be quiet without fear of evil.
evidence_phrases: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel.
To know wisdom and instruction.
To discern the words of understanding.
To receive instruction in wise dealing, In righteousness and justice and equity.
But whoso hearkeneth unto me shall dwell securely, And shall be quiet without fear of evil.

title: Seeking wisdom and fleeing evil
summary_text: The chapter urges the son to treasure wisdom and call out for understanding. If he seeks discernment like hidden treasure, God will guard him from evil men and crooked paths. The end of the chapter makes the contrast plain: the righteous remain in the land, but the wicked are cut off. Seeking wisdom is shown as a matter of life and safety.
key_points: The son is told to receive the words and lay up the commandments.
Wisdom and understanding must be sought like treasure.
The Lord gives wisdom and stores up sound counsel for the upright.
Evil men and strange women lead to dark paths and destruction.
The wicked are cut off, but the upright dwell in the land.
evidence_phrases: receive my words, And lay up my commandments with thee
cry after discernment
The Lord giveth wisdom
the wicked shall be cut off from the land
the upright shall dwell in it

title: Trusting the Lord and honoring wisdom
summary_text: My son, forget not my law. Trusting the Lord is the center of the chapter, and obedience is tied to life and peace. Wisdom is practical, generous, and steady. But shame shall be the promotion of fools.
key_points: My son, forget not my law.
But let thy heart keep my commandments.
Let not kindness and truth forsake thee.
Bind them about thy neck.
Write them upon the tablet of thy heart.
evidence_phrases: My son, forget not my law.
But let thy heart keep my commandments.
Let not kindness and truth forsake thee.
Bind them about thy neck.
Write them upon the tablet of thy heart.

title: A father's path of wisdom
summary_text: Hear, my sons, the instruction of a father, And attend to know understanding. A father hands down a clear path for the child to keep, stressing guarded speech, disciplined steps, and a heart kept from evil. Remove thy foot from evil.
key_points: Hear, my sons, the instruction of a father, And attend to know understanding.
For I give you good doctrine.
Forsake ye not my law.
Make level the path of thy feet, And let all thy ways be established.
Turn not to the right hand nor to the left.
evidence_phrases: Hear, my sons, the instruction of a father, And attend to know understanding.
For I give you good doctrine.
Forsake ye not my law.
Make level the path of thy feet, And let all thy ways be established.
Turn not to the right hand nor to the left.

title: Warning against adultery
summary_text: My son, attend unto my wisdom. The chapter warns about sin that looks pleasant but ends in loss. Wisdom protects covenant faithfulness, work, and clean living. And in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray.
key_points: My son, attend unto my wisdom.
Incline thine ear to my understanding.
That thou mayest preserve discretion, And that thy lips may keep knowledge.
He shall die for lack of instruction.
And in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray.

title: Practical warnings and the Lord's hatred of evil
summary_text: My son, if thou art become surety for thy neighbor, If thou hast stricken thy hands for a.... Practical warnings pile up against surety, sloth, deception, and sexual sin, ending with a list of things the Lord hates. Neither will he rest content, though thou givest many gifts.

title: Seduction and the strange woman
summary_text: My son, keep my words, And lay up my commandments with thee. A vivid story of seduction shows how folly flatters and then kills. The chapter is a pastoral alarm bell for the heart. Her house is the way to Sheol, Going down to the chambers of death.
key_points: My son, keep my words, And lay up my commandments with thee.
Keep my commandments and live.
And my law as the apple of thine eye.
Bind them upon thy fingers.
Write them upon the tablet of thy heart.
evidence_phrases: My son, keep my words, And lay up my commandments with thee.

And my law as the apple of thine eye.

title: Wisdom's public call
summary_text: Doth not wisdom cry, And understanding put forth her voice. Wisdom speaks in public and invites the simple to hear life, righteousness, and truth. Her words stand beside God's own ordering of creation. All they that hate me love death.
key_points: Doth not wisdom cry, And understanding put forth her voice.
For whoso findeth me findeth life, And shall obtain favor of Jehovah.
But he that sinneth against me wrongeth his own soul.
All they that hate me love death.

title: Wisdom and folly invite
summary_text: Wisdom hath builded her house. Wisdom and folly each invite the simple to a meal, but their houses lead to opposite ends. The chapter forces a choice between life and death. That her guests are in the depths of Sheol.
key_points: Wisdom hath builded her house.
She hath hewn out her seven pillars.

title: Wise son and foolish son
summary_text: The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son maketh a glad father. This collection keeps contrasting righteousness and wickedness in speech, work, and wealth. The sayings show how a steady life under God looks in practical detail. But the mouth of the wicked speaketh perverseness.
key_points: The proverbs of Solomon.
A wise son maketh a glad father.
But a foolish son is the heaviness of his mother.
Treasures of wickedness profit nothing.
But righteousness delivereth from death.
evidence_phrases: The proverbs of Solomon.

title: Righteousness and the wicked
summary_text: These also are proverbs of Solomon, which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah copied out. Hezekiah's copied proverbs emphasize humility, wise speech, and patient leadership. The sayings keep pointing to self-knowledge and restraint. He whose spirit is without restraint Is like a city that is broken down and without walls.

title: Friendship and counsel
summary_text: The chapter warns against boasting, self-confidence, and unchecked pride. It values honest friendship, careful correction, and practical prudence in ordinary life. Hezekiah's copied proverbs keep reminding the reader that reputation is best received, not announced. The closing image of care for the household ties wisdom to patient stewardship.
key_points: The chapter begins by warning not to boast about tomorrow.
It says that another person should praise you rather than your own mouth.
Friendship sharpens friendship, as iron sharpens iron.
A good name and careful observation matter more than self-promotion.
The last verses turn wisdom toward household care and daily responsibility.

title: Lemuel's mother and the excellent woman
summary_text: The words of king Lemuel. His mother warns him about drunkenness, injustice, and the burden of ruling, then the chapter turns to the capable woman who fears the Lord. Her work, speech, and care bless her household and honor her in the gates. Give her of the fruit of her hands; And let her works praise her in the gates.
key_points: The words of king Lemuel, the oracle which his mother taught him.
It warns kings against wine and against perverting justice.
Open thy mouth, judge righteously, And plead the cause of the poor and needy.
